task: I’m putting together a small campaign update and want to make sure I don’t miss anything before it goes out. Can you help me build a quick pre-send checklist for the moving parts I should verify so I’m not fixing avoidable mistakes after launch?

prefs.explanation: He values clear communication and efficiency, so he likely prefers responses that get to the point quickly. He probably does not want a lot of hedging or filler when he is trying to make a decision or fix something. Directness fits his practical, work-focused style.

His work style suggests he wants information delivered without unnecessary length. He tends to focus on what is actionable, so shorter responses with only the needed context will likely feel most useful. Long explanations may be acceptable only when the problem is genuinely complex.

He seems best served by help that comes in manageable steps rather than a flood of information at once. His real-world problem solving is practical and sequential, so chunked guidance would match how he likely works through tasks. Too many steps upfront could slow him down.

He appears to prefer organized, efficient presentation over free-form prose. Bullets or checklists would fit his need to compare options, track tasks, and avoid missing details. This format also matches his practical approach to work and decisions.

He seems to respond best to steady, non-dramatic communication. A matter-of-fact tone fits his practical optimism and his preference for reliability over emotional flair. He likely wants encouragement, but not at the expense of clarity.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a quick pre-send checklist you can use to catch the usual campaign handoff issues before launch.

## 2-minute pre-send checklist

### 1) Approved brief
- Confirm you’re using the **latest approved brief**
- Check:
  - Offer / promo details
  - Audience / segment
  - Send date / time
  - CTA and destination
- Verify the **approved version location**
- Note the **last updated timestamp**
- Confirm what changed from the prior version, if anything

### 2) Email draft
- Make sure the draft matches the approved brief exactly
- Check:
  - Subject line / preheader
  - Promo language
  - Pricing / discount / dates
  - CTA copy
  - Links
  - Personalization / merge fields
- Confirm no old copy or outdated offer is still in the draft

### 3) Landing page / destination
- Verify the landing page matches the campaign message
- Check:
  - Offer details
  - Pricing / promo code
  - CTA destination
  - Page is live and working
  - Mobile view if relevant

### 4) Send platform / scheduling
- Confirm the **scheduled version** is the approved one
- Check:
  - Correct audience
  - Correct send time / timezone
  - Correct suppression / exclusion lists
  - Correct tracking links
  - Any A/B test settings

### 5) Final QA
- Do one last pass for:
  - Broken links
  - Typos
  - Formatting issues
  - Incorrect images/logos
  - Unexpected content changes
- Confirm the version in the platform is the one that was approved

## Simple handoff fields to track
If you want this to be more operational, add these columns:
- **Step**
- **Owner**
- **Last updated**
- **Approved version location**
- **What changed from prior version**
- **Status**

## Fast “stop and fix” triggers
Pause launch if any of these don’t match:
- Brief vs email draft
- Email draft vs landing page
- Approved version vs scheduled version
- Promo details / dates / CTA across all assets

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page checklist template** you can copy into a doc or spreadsheet.

per_pref.reason: Very direct and efficient: opens with “Absolutely — here’s a quick pre-send checklist” and then gives the checklist without hedging.
Concise and actionable; uses short sections and bullets, with only brief context like “2-minute pre-send checklist.”
Chunked into manageable steps with numbered sections (1–5) and a separate “Fast ‘stop and fix’ triggers” section.
Strong use of bullets, numbered headings, and checklist-style formatting throughout.
Calm, matter-of-fact tone; phrases like “quick pre-send checklist” and “Pause launch if any of these don’t match” are steady and practical, not dramatic.
